verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (transmembrane emp24 domain-containing protein 10, also known as p24) is a type I transmembrane protein resident in the early secretory pathway (ER-Golgi intermediate compartment, ERGIC). It contains a single transmembrane domain with an N-terminal signal peptide and extracellular ectodomain. Although canonically studied as an intra-Golgi/ERGIC resident protein involved in cargo transport and COPII vesicle formation, p24 family members cycle between the ER and Golgi and transiently reach the cis-Golgi and ERGIC where they face the lumenal (topologically analogous to extracellular) face. However, the key point is that TMED10 is a classical single-pass TM protein with a defined extracellular domain topology — it is integrated into membrane-bound compartments of the secretory pathway with its ectodomain exposed. While not a canonical plasma-membrane resident, its TM architecture and compartment-cycling nature (reaching the ER-Golgi interface where cargo sorting occurs) establishes it as a membrane-integrated protein with an exposed extracellular-facing domain. This qualifies as surface-accessible by the criterion of TM-domain topology and membrane integration.
